heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Norman Jewison. Written by Stirling Silliphant, based on the 1965 John Ball novel.
clean: Directed by Norman Jewison. Written by Stirling Silliphant, based on the 1965 John Ball novel.

heading: Virgil Tibbs
raw: Now listen, hear me good, Mama. Please. Don't make me have to send you to jail... There's white time in jail and there's colored time in jail. The worst kind of time you can do is colored time.
clean: Now listen, hear me good, Mama. Please. Don't make me have to send you to jail... There's white time in jail and there's colored time in jail. The worst kind of time you can do is colored time.

heading: Chief Gillespie
raw: I got the motive which is murder and the body which is dead!
clean: I got the motive which is murder and the body which is dead!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Chief Gillespie: Got a name, boy?
clean: Got a name, boy?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Virgil Tibbs.
clean: Virgil Tibbs.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Virgil? [Laughing] Well, I don't think we're going to have any trouble, are we, Virgil?
clean: Virgil? Well, I don't think we're going to have any trouble, are we, Virgil?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: No trouble at all.
clean: No trouble at all.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: You can go now, Sam. [Deputy Sam Wood leaves the room] Whatcha hit him with?
clean: You can go now, Sam. Whatcha hit him with?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Hit whom?
clean: Hit whom?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: "Whom"? "Whom"? Well, you a Northern boy? What's a Northern boy like you doing all the way down here?
clean: "Whom"? "Whom"? Well, you a Northern boy? What's a Northern boy like you doing all the way down here?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: I was waiting for the train.
clean: I was waiting for the train.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: There ain't no trains this time in the morning.
clean: There ain't no trains this time in the morning.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Tuesdays only 4:05 to Memphis.
clean: Tuesdays only 4:05 to Memphis.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: You say. [train horn blowing] Well, alright. You say right. Now, look. I try to run a nice, clean, safe town here. A town where a man can sneeze and not get his brains beat out. You follow me?
clean: You say. Well, alright. You say right. Now, look. I try to run a nice, clean, safe town here. A town where a man can sneeze and not get his brains beat out. You follow me?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Why don't you tell me how you killed Mr. Colbert, and I promise you you're gonna feel a whole lot better.
clean: Why don't you tell me how you killed Mr. Colbert, and I promise you you're gonna feel a whole lot better.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: I earned that money, ten hours a day, seven days a week.
clean: I earned that money, ten hours a day, seven days a week.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Colored can't earn that kind of money [a few hundred dollars], boy. Hell, that's more than I earn in a month! Now where did you earn it?!
clean: Colored can't earn that kind of money , boy. Hell, that's more than I earn in a month! Now where did you earn it?!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Philadelphia.
clean: Philadelphia.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Mississippi?
clean: Mississippi?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Pennsylvania.
clean: Pennsylvania.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Now just what do you do up there in little old Pennsylvania to earn that kind of money?
clean: Now just what do you do up there in little old Pennsylvania to earn that kind of money?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: I'm a police officer.
clean: I'm a police officer.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Wood!
clean: Wood!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Wood: [Enters the office] Yes, sir?
clean: Yes, sir?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Did you question this man before you brought him in?
clean: Did you question this man before you brought him in?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Wood: No, sir.
clean: No, sir.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Would you mind takin' a look at that?
clean: Would you mind takin' a look at that?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Yeah! Oh, YEAH! Alright, now, I'll check on this wise city boy from Philadelphia; you take 'im outside and hold 'im.
clean: Yeah! Oh, YEAH! Alright, now, I'll check on this wise city boy from Philadelphia; you take 'im outside and hold 'im.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: May I suggest you call my chief rather than send a wire or anything, I mean, it would be quicker, and I'll pay for the call.
clean: May I suggest you call my chief rather than send a wire or anything, I mean, it would be quicker, and I'll pay for the call.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: You hear him? You hear him say he'll pay for the call? How much they pay you to do their police work?
clean: You hear him? You hear him say he'll pay for the call? How much they pay you to do their police work?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: A hundred and sixty-two dollars, and thirty-nine cents per week.
clean: A hundred and sixty-two dollars, and thirty-nine cents per week.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: [Whistles] A hundred and sixty-two dollars and thirty-nine cents a week? Well, boy! Sam, you take him outside but treat him easy, because a man that makes a hundred and sixty-two dollars and thirty-nine cents a week, man, we do not want to ruffle him!
clean: A hundred and sixty-two dollars and thirty-nine cents a week? Well, boy! Sam, you take him outside but treat him easy, because a man that makes a hundred and sixty-two dollars and thirty-nine cents a week, man, we do not want to ruffle him!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: So, you're their number one homicide expert.
clean: So, you're their number one homicide expert.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Boy, I bet you get to look at a lot of dead bodies, don't'cha?
clean: Boy, I bet you get to look at a lot of dead bodies, don't'cha?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Lots.
clean: Lots.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Well?
clean: Well?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Well, what?
clean: Well, what?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Well, I just thought that, maybe, uh, maybe you wouldn't mind takin' a look at this one?
clean: Well, I just thought that, maybe, uh, maybe you wouldn't mind takin' a look at this one?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: No, thanks.
clean: No, thanks.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Well, why not, expert?
clean: Well, why not, expert?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Because I've got a train to catch!
clean: Because I've got a train to catch!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Now, wait a minute! That train don't leave till twelve o'clock noon! They pay you a hundred and sixty-two dollars and thirty-nine cents a week just to look at bodies- why can't you look at this one?!
clean: Now, wait a minute! That train don't leave till twelve o'clock noon! They pay you a hundred and sixty-two dollars and thirty-nine cents a week just to look at bodies- why can't you look at this one?!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Why don't you look at it for yourself?
clean: Why don't you look at it for yourself?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Because I'm not an expert! Officer!
clean: Because I'm not an expert! Officer!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. Colbert: I came by to make it as clear as I possibly can: that I do not want the Negro officer taken off this case.
clean: I came by to make it as clear as I possibly can: that I do not want the Negro officer taken off this case.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mayor Schubert: Negro officer?
clean: Negro officer?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Yeah, well he, uh... he comes from up North, you see, and he was, uh, kinda... passing through...
clean: Yeah, well he, uh... he comes from up North, you see, and he was, uh, kinda... passing through...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. Colbert: I don't care what he is. If it wasn't for him, your impartial chief of police would still have the wrong man behind bars. I want that officer given a free hand, otherwise I will pack up my husband's engineers... and leave you... to yourselves.
clean: I don't care what he is. If it wasn't for him, your impartial chief of police would still have the wrong man behind bars. I want that officer given a free hand, otherwise I will pack up my husband's engineers... and leave you... to yourselves.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: When I examined the deceased, it was obvious that the fatal blow was struck from an angle of seventeen degrees to the right, which makes it almost certain the person who did it was right-handed.
clean: When I examined the deceased, it was obvious that the fatal blow was struck from an angle of seventeen degrees to the right, which makes it almost certain the person who did it was right-handed.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: So what?
clean: So what?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Shagbag: Ol' Harv's left-handed, Chief. Everyone in town knows that.
clean: Ol' Harv's left-handed, Chief. Everyone in town knows that.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Wood: Yeah, that's what we figured out, Chief, uh-huh, Harvey's lefty.
clean: Yeah, that's what we figured out, Chief, uh-huh, Harvey's lefty.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Well, you're pretty sure of yourself, ain't you, Virgil? Virgil—that's a funny name for a nigger boy that comes from Philadelphia! What do they call you up there?
clean: Well, you're pretty sure of yourself, ain't you, Virgil? Virgil-that's a funny name for a nigger boy that comes from Philadelphia! What do they call you up there?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: They call me Mr. Tibbs!
clean: They call me Mr. Tibbs!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: "Mr. Tibbs"? Well Mr. Wood, take Mr. Tibbs, take him down to the depot, and I mean boy right now!
clean: "Mr. Tibbs"? Well Mr. Wood, take Mr. Tibbs, take him down to the depot, and I mean boy right now!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Note: The bold quote is ranked #16 in the American Film Institute's list of the top 100 movie quotations in American cinema.
clean: The bold quote is ranked #16 in the American Film Institute's list of the top 100 movie quotations in American cinema.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Endicott: Let me understand this. You two came here... to question me?
clean: Let me understand this. You two came here... to question me?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Endicott: Gillespie?
clean: Gillespie?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Yeah.
clean: Yeah.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Endicott: You saw it.
clean: You saw it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: I saw it.
clean: I saw it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Endicott: Well, what are you gonna do about it?
clean: Well, what are you gonna do about it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: I don't know.
clean: I don't know.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Endicott: I'll remember that. [to Tibbs] There was a time... when I could've had you shot.
clean: I'll remember that. There was a time... when I could've had you shot.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mayor Schubert: Bill... what's made you change your mind about Tibbs?
clean: Bill... what's made you change your mind about Tibbs?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Who says I have?
clean: Who says I have?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mayor Schubert: Last Chief we had... he'd have shot Tibbs one second after he slapped Endicott, claimed self-defense.
clean: Last Chief we had... he'd have shot Tibbs one second after he slapped Endicott, claimed self-defense.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: I'm going home, man.
clean: I'm going home, man.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: They're your people!
clean: They're your people!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Not mine! Yours! You made this scene.
clean: Not mine! Yours! You made this scene.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Look, you want me to beg you, is that it?
clean: Look, you want me to beg you, is that it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: Look, I've had your town- up to here!
clean: Look, I've had your town- up to here!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gillespie: Boy, it would give me a world of satisfaction to horse whip you, Virgil.
clean: Boy, it would give me a world of satisfaction to horse whip you, Virgil.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tibbs: [laughing] My father used to say that. He even did a couple of times.
clean: My father used to say that. He even did a couple of times.
